claims.claim: `IncludeSecuredItems` is a property on `ContentQueryExecutionOptions` that controls whether secured items are included in query results
claims.location: Section: Secured content in query results, paragraph 1 (line 302)
claims.evidence: ContentQueryExecutionOptions.cs line 17: `public bool IncludeSecuredItems { get; set; } = false;` with XML doc: 'Indicates whether the secured items should be included. The default value is false.'
claims.docFact: AREA-006: Query results include ContentItemRequiredMemberRoleNames when IncludeSecuredItems is true and items are role-restricted

claims.claim: When `IncludeSecuredItems` is `true`, the query executor retrieves both public and secured content items
claims.location: Section: Secured content in query results, paragraph 1 (line 302)
claims.evidence: ContentQueryExecutionOptions.cs line 14-17: XML doc states 'Indicates whether the secured items should be included.' Default is false. ContentQueryExecutor.cs line 127: `builder.Build(executionContext, executionOptions, cancellationToken)` passes options to query builder.
claims.docFact: AREA-006: Query results include ContentItemRequiredMemberRoleNames when IncludeSecuredItems is true and items are role-restricted

claims.claim: The system automatically populates the `ContentItemRequiredMemberRoleNames` property on each result's `IContentQueryDataContainer`
claims.location: Section: Secured content in query results, paragraph 1 (line 302)
claims.evidence: ContentItemRolesResultProcessor.cs lines 32-54: PostProcessQueryResult iterates all containers (including linked items) and calls SetRoleNames on each. ContentQueryExecutor.cs lines 158,164-173: PostProcessDataContainers calls all registered IContentQueryResultProcessor instances after query execution.
claims.docFact: AREA-006: ContentItemRolesResultProcessor runs as post-processing step after query execution

claims.claim: `ContentItemRequiredMemberRoleNames` contains the code names of the required roles
claims.location: Section: Secured content in query results, paragraph 1 (line 302)
claims.evidence: IContentQueryDataContainer.cs line 60: property type is `IReadOnlyCollection<string>`. ContentItemRolesResultProcessor.cs line 48: calls `contentItemMemberRoleRetriever.GetMemberRoleCodeNames(contentItemIds)` confirming code names.

claims.evidence: IContentQueryDataContainer.cs line 60: `IReadOnlyCollection<string> ContentItemRequiredMemberRoleNames => GetValue<IReadOnlyCollection<string>>(nameof(ContentItemRequiredMemberRoleNames));` — defined as a default interface member.
claims.docFact: AREA-006: IContentQueryDataContainer.ContentItemRequiredMemberRoleNames: IReadOnlyCollection<string> — new property with default interface implementation

claims.claim: Returns an empty collection for public items
claims.location: Section: Secured content in query results, code example comment (line 331)
claims.evidence: ContentQueryDataContainer.cs lines 36-38: `get => (requiredMemberRoleNames ?? []).ToList().AsReadOnly();` — when null (default for unsecured items), returns empty collection. ContentItemRolesResultProcessor.cs lines 66-69: SetRoleNames only sets value when `roleNames.Any()`.
claims.docFact: AREA-006: For unsecured items, returns an empty collection

claims.claim: For items that are not secured, `ContentItemRequiredMemberRoleNames` returns an empty collection
claims.location: Section: Secured content in query results, paragraph after code (line 346)
claims.evidence: Same evidence: ContentQueryDataContainer.cs line 38 returns empty readonly list when internal field is null.
claims.docFact: AREA-006: For unsecured items, returns an empty collection

claims.claim: The system resolves role data using lazy loading — no additional database queries are executed unless at least one item in the result is secured
claims.location: Section: Secured content in query results, paragraph after code (line 346)
claims.evidence: ContentItemRolesResultProcessor.cs lines 36-40: `if (!allContainers.Any(c => c.ContentItemIsSecured)) { return; }` — returns immediately without DB call when no items are secured.
claims.docFact: AREA-006: Lazy loading — role data only fetched from DB when at least one item has ContentItemIsSecured = true

claims.claim: Linked item support exists for ContentItemRequiredMemberRoleNames
claims.location: Section: Secured content in query results, cross-reference (line 348)
claims.evidence: ContentItemRolesResultProcessor.cs lines 73-89: GetAllItemsIncludingLinkedItems uses BFS queue to enumerate all containers including linked items via LinkedItemsByField.
claims.docFact: AREA-006: Works recursively through linked items
